free_text: Fine Structure in the Giant Resonance Region and the
Collective Dipole Spin-Flip Excitation in Pb-208.
Some Additions For Sample and Method
Targets were Prepared by Deposition in Vacuum of Pb-208
Enriched to 99.1% onto 20 Mug/Cm**2 C-12 Foil. For
Targets of Approximatelly 1 Mg/Cm**2 a Resolution of
20 KeV Could be Achieved and this was Limited by the
Focal Plane Detector and Beam Resolution. Most Data
At 45 MeV were Taken Using a Target of 5.4 Mg/Cm**2
Thickness Which Limited the Resolution to About 35 KeV.
By Using a Closed Vacuum Transport System the Oxygen
Contamination was Kept So Low in the Spectra That No
Peaks Due to Oxygen were Observable.
Spectrum of Alpha-Particles from the Pb-208(A,A')
Reaction at 48 MeV and 14 Degree was Measured.
The  Scattered  Particles were Detected in a Delay Line
Gas Counter on the Focal Plane of an Enge Split-Pole
Scintillator was Used to Eliminate Slit-Scattered
